an3.57:1.1: Then the wanderer Vacchagotta approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached, he exchanged greetings with the *Bhagavā*.
an3.57:1.10: Gifts should be given to my disciples alone, not to other disciples.
an3.57:1.11: What is given to me alone bears great fruit, not what is given to others.
an3.57:1.12: What is given to my disciples alone bears great fruit, not what is given to other disciples"' —
an3.57:1.13: do they report what was actually said by Master Gotama? Do they not misrepresent Master Gotama with what is false and untrue? Do they explain in accordance with the *dhamma*, and does no legitimate consequence of their assertion lead to a blameworthy position?
an3.57:1.14: For we have no wish to misrepresent Master Gotama."
an3.57:1.2: Having exchanged courteous and memorable greetings, he sat to one side. Seated to one side, the wanderer Vacchagotta said this to the *Bhagavā*:
an3.57:1.3: "I have heard this, Master Gotama, that the ascetic Gotama says:
an3.57:1.4: 'Gifts should be given to me alone, not to others;
an3.57:1.5: gifts should be given to my disciples alone, not to other disciples;
an3.57:1.6: what is given to me alone bears great fruit, not what is given to others;
an3.57:1.7: what is given to my disciples alone bears great fruit, not what is given to other disciples.'
an3.57:1.8: Those who say this, Master Gotama — 'The ascetic Gotama says:
an3.57:1.9: "Gifts should be given to me alone, not to others.
an3.57:10.3: the burden laid down, freed from bonds,
an3.57:12.2: of poor wisdom and without learning,
an3.57:12.3: give their gifts to those outside
an3.57:12.4: and do not attend on the good.
an3.57:13.1: Those who attend on the good,
an3.57:13.2: on the wise, honored by the steadfast,
an3.57:13.3: whose faith in the Well-Gone One
an3.57:14.1: they go to the realm of the gods,
an3.57:14.2: or are reborn here in a good family;
an3.57:2.3: "Gifts should be given to me alone …
an3.57:2.4: not what is given to other disciples"' —
an3.57:2.5: they do not report what was said by me. They misrepresent me with what is false and untrue.
an3.57:2.6: Whoever, Vaccha, prevents another from giving makes himself an obstacle to three things, an obstructer of three.
an3.57:2.8: He makes himself an obstacle to the merit of the donor, an obstacle to the gains of the recipients, and before all else, he has already wounded and harmed himself.
an3.57:2.9: Whoever, Vaccha, prevents another from giving makes himself an obstacle to these three things, an obstructer of three.
an3.57:3.1: But this is what I say, Vaccha:
an3.57:3.2: 'Even for the creatures living in a gutter or a drain — when someone throws away the rinsings of a pot or a bowl there,
an3.57:3.3: "may the creatures there be sustained by this" — even from that, Vaccha, I say merit accrues.'
an3.57:3.4: How much more so when it comes to human beings.
an3.57:3.5: And yet, Vaccha, I say that what is given to a virtuous person bears great fruit — not so with one of poor virtue — and that person has abandoned five factors and is endowed with five factors.
an3.57:4.1: Which five factors have been abandoned?
an3.57:4.2: Sensual desire has been abandoned, ill will has been abandoned, sloth-and-torpor has been abandoned, restlessness-and-worry has been abandoned, doubt has been abandoned.
an3.57:4.3: These five factors have been abandoned.
an3.57:5.1: And with which five factors is he endowed?
an3.57:5.2: He is endowed with the aggregate of virtue of one beyond training, endowed with the aggregate of *samādhi* of one beyond training, endowed with the aggregate of discernment [*paññā*] of one beyond training, endowed with the aggregate of liberation of one beyond training, endowed with the aggregate of the knowledge and vision of liberation of one beyond training;
an3.57:5.3: he is endowed with these five factors.
an3.57:5.4: Thus I say: what is given to one who has abandoned five factors and is endowed with five factors bears great fruit.
an3.57:6.1: Among black cows and white cows,
an3.57:6.3: spotted ones and those of uniform color,
an3.57:7.3: fit to carry loads, endowed with strength,
an3.57:7.5: it is that one they yoke to the burden;
an3.57:7.6: they do not pass it over on account of its color.
an3.57:9.2: one of good conduct is born, tamed,
